text: If you're planning to be outside much today, keep an eye on that hazy sky, the wildfire smoke drifting down from Canada has bumped our air quality to moderate, and it'll hang around into the afternoon before storms clear it out some. Otherwise it's a mostly sunny day with a high near 82, but the models are arguing about how wet it gets: our forecasters and the American model like a lower rain chance, while the European model's much wetter, so I'd keep the rain jacket handy for those afternoon and evening thunderstorms. On the lake, southwest wind builds into a stiff 15 to 25 knots out of the northwest tonight, so get your boating done well before evening. Tonight clears out and turns comfortably cool, setting up a genuinely gorgeous Friday, sunny, high near 81, with none of today's smoke or storm worries. Good window on the water or trails today before roughly 3pm; after that, watch the sky and don't push your luck out on the broad lake.
